name: Senolytics Coverage Repair

scope.question: Which known source-completeness gaps for Senolytics need repair, and does resolving them change the coverage verdict or public boundaries?

summary: Coverage repair staged source, study, finding, intervention, registry-watch, review, animal-anchor, support-map, and coverage-assessment records for Senolytics. The repair adds D+Q discovery and old-mouse healthspan/lifespan anchors, direct human D+Q IPF, diabetic kidney disease, postmenopausal bone, and cognition/mobility records, a translation-review boundary, fisetin discovery context, active no-results fisetin frailty and vascular-function registries, an active no-results D+Q Alzheimer's phase 2 registry, and a staged outlook support map. The staged outlook keeps the track at moderate-confidence human_biomarker_signal with mixed momentum rather than upgrading to human_functional_benefit.

materiality_decision.rationale: The repair materially changes the public support map and boundary language without changing the stage: direct human biomarker support is now auditable, functional signals are visible but still low-confidence, and current registry activity is represented as no-results watch context rather than benefit evidence.

search_log.summary: Confirmed source metadata and abstracts for the D+Q discovery, fisetin discovery, old-mouse healthspan/lifespan, first-in-human IPF, DKD biomarker, randomized IPF tolerability, postmenopausal bone RCT, 2025 cognition/mobility pilot, and senolytic-drugs translation review records. The SToMP-AD protocol and 2026 murine DKD source were recorded as context but not staged to keep the repair bounded.

search_log.query: senolytic OR fisetin OR dasatinib quercetin plus direct checks for NCT03430037, NCT03675724, NCT06133634, NCT04685590, NCT05422885, NCT04313634, NCT02874989, and NCT02848131
search_log.summary: Confirmed current status, enrollment, dates, endpoint scope, and results status. AFFIRM, AFFIRM-LITE, fisetin vascular function, and SToMP-AD phase 2 are no-results watch items. NCT05422885 and NCT04313634 have result-bearing paper support; NCT02874989 and NCT02848131 have paper support but no posted registry results in the API view.

search_log.source_name: Local public records and coverage assessment
search_log.query: Senolytics outlook, May 30 surveillance session, June 11 coverage assessment, existing D+Q intervention, existing D+Q endothelial TIF records, and neighboring senomorphic, immune-clearance, and telomere-protection support maps
search_log.summary: Compared the prior thin support map against local records and adjacent tracks. The repair prioritizes direct Senolytics support-map normalization, human biomarker and functional boundaries, trial-watch records, and taxonomy separation from senomorphics, immune clearance, and telomere-protection interpretations.

trial_watch_checks.summary: Staged as an enrolling-by-invitation no-results fisetin frailty trial in older women with a 6-minute-walk primary endpoint.
Staged as an enrolling-by-invitation no-results fisetin older-adults trial with a blood inflammation marker primary endpoint.
Staged as an active-not-recruiting no-results fisetin vascular-function trial in older adults.
Staged as an active-not-recruiting no-results randomized phase 2 D+Q Alzheimer's disease safety and feasibility trial.
Staged the peer-reviewed 2025 EBioMedicine pilot as result-bearing D+Q cognition, mobility, safety, and biomarker context.
Staged the peer-reviewed Nature Medicine phase 2 bone RCT as a mixed human biomarker and safety boundary record.
The registry remains completed without posted API results, but result-bearing IPF publications support the staged human functional and randomized feasibility findings.
The registry remains no-results in the API view; the peer-reviewed 2019 DKD pilot provides the staged human senescent-cell biomarker signal.

excluded_sources.title: Senolytic Therapy to Modulate the Progression of Alzheimer's Disease (SToMP-AD): A Pilot Clinical Trial
excluded_sources.reason: Protocol and rationale source for early D+Q Alzheimer's work; the repair instead stages the result-bearing 2025 cognition/mobility pilot and the current randomized SToMP-AD phase 2 registry.

excluded_sources.title: Senolytics, dasatanib plus quercetin, reduce kidney inflammation, senescent cell abundance, and injury while restoring geroprotective factors in murine diabetic kidney disease
excluded_sources.reason: Recent 2026 murine DKD follow-up is direct but not necessary for this bounded repair after staging the human DKD biomarker pilot, old-mouse healthspan/lifespan anchor, and existing aged-mouse endothelial TIF record.

excluded_sources.title: Corrigendum to 'Senolytics decrease senescent cells in humans'
excluded_sources.reason: Corrigendum was noted but does not require a separate staged public support record.

excluded_sources.title: ALSENLITE: Senolytics for Alzheimer's Disease
excluded_sources.reason: Direct but open-label no-results Alzheimer's D+Q registry; the staged bundle keeps the AD branch bounded to the 2025 result-bearing pilot and the randomized SToMP-AD phase 2 registry.

excluded_sources.reference: COVID, oncology, osteoarthritis, endometrial, and supplement-brand senolytic registry matches
excluded_sources.reason: ClinicalTrials.gov senolytic/fisetin/D+Q search returned many disease-treatment or commercial-adjacent records; these were not staged because the repair target was aging-relevant Senolytics coverage for D+Q and fisetin core branches.

notes: The staged repair intentionally keeps evidence_stage at human_biomarker_signal because the direct controlled human evidence is biomarker-heavy and mixed.
No-results registry entries are represented as regulatory/program-update findings with inconclusive direction.
Month-only ClinicalTrials.gov completion dates were normalized to the first day of the month in structured date fields and preserved as month-level timing in notes.
The bundle stages public record candidates only and does not approve or publish them.
next_actions: Run source-fidelity, interpretation/forecast, taxonomy-mapping, and safety-limitations review for the staged Senolytics coverage-repair bundle.

If approved and published, return Senolytics to ordinary surveillance with special watch on NCT03430037, NCT03675724, NCT06133634, NCT04685590, NCT04785300, and follow-up result postings for completed D+Q trials.
